records.ety: [L. implexus, p. p. of implectere to infold; pref. im- in + plectere to plait: cf. F implexe.]

quotes: The fable of every poem is . . . simple or implex. it is called simple when there is no change of fortune in it; implex, when the fortune of the chief actor changes from bad to good, or from good to bad.
